operating_state: Revenue increased 36% to $11.3 billion driven by Mounjaro ($3.1 billion), Zepbound ($1.2 billion), and Verzenio ($1.3 billion), partially offset by Trulicity declines. Incretin medicine growth reflected production increases improving channel dynamics and stocking levels, with Mounjaro and Zepbound U.S. sales benefiting from high-teens to mid-20s percent channel stocking. International revenue grew 25% led by Mounjaro and Verzenio expansion.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded to 80.8% from 78.3% driven by higher realized prices and favorable product mix toward incretin medicines. Research and development expenses increased 15% reflecting continued portfolio investment. Marketing, selling, and administrative expenses rose 10% primarily from launch investments and personnel expansion.
material_events: Donanemab received FDA approval for early Alzheimer's disease treatment in July 2024. Asset impairment and restructuring charges of $435 million related to anticipated litigation payments. The company issued $6.5 billion in fixed-rate notes in February 2024 for general business purposes including debt refinancing. CFO Anat Ashkenazi resigned in June 2024 to pursue opportunities outside pharmaceuticals.
macro_exposures: Inflation Reduction Act selected Jardiance for government price-setting effective 2026, with additional significant products expected in future years accelerating revenue erosion. Supply chain constraints continue affecting incretin medicine production despite improved balance between supply and demand. Foreign currency headwinds reduced consolidated revenue growth by approximately 1 percentage point.
forward_outlook: Manufacturing capacity expansion continues through 2024 with additional capacity operational over several years to meet incretin demand. Zepbound 2.5mg and 5mg single-dose vials planned for Q3 2024 U.S. launch. The company announced agreement to acquire Morphic Holding for approximately $3.2 billion, subject to customary closing conditions including majority share tender.
uncertainty_profile: Incretin medicine sales growth remains constrained by production capacity with periodic supply tightness expected for certain presentations. Pending regulatory reviews include tirzepatide for obstructive sleep apnea and lebrikizumab resubmission in the U.S. Multiple Phase 3 trials ongoing across cardiometabolic, immunology, and neuroscience portfolios with regulatory submissions anticipated.
